Rule.name: Privileged Item Selector Example
Rule.Description: An example rule that shows how you can filter the list of privileged items available to add based on the users attributes or classifications on the privileged item.

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: The log object associated with the SailPointContext.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: requester
Argument.Description: Identity that is making the request.

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: The PAM application the container is associated with.

Argument.name: container
Argument.Description: The container managed attribute that the privileged items would get added to.

Argument.name: filter
Argument.Description: A Filter object that will be used to search for accessible privileged items.

Rule.name: AW-Approval_Assignment_Rule_Custom_Approval_Framework
Rule.Description:
This example rule switches all of the owners to spadmin. The idea of this rule is to allow some customization of the approvals so the ownership can be calculated for cases where you might use and extended attribute or some other means outside our default mechanisms to derive the owner of the Approval.
This rule is typically configured on the Approval Step in the LCM Provisioning Workflow or can be set on any steps based on the "Provisioning Approval" step library.
Since 6.2

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: The log object associated with the SailPointContext.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: approvals
Argument.Description: List of approvals generated by the approvalScheme, maybe null if there were no approvals enabled.

Argument.name: approvalSet
Argument.Description: Representation of the cart, also found on the approvals generated by the default schemes.

Argument.name: newApprovals
Argument.Description: Return a lIst of Workflow.Approval objects that should be included during the approval process.

Rule.name: ACCESSWARDEN-IdentityCreationRule
Rule.Description:
Identity creation rules are used to set attributes on new Identity objects when they are created. New identities may be created during the aggregation of application accounts, or optionally created after pass-through authentication.
One common operation is to change the name property of the identity when the default application name is complex (such as a directory DN).
Another common operation is to assign a set of initial capabilities based on the attributes pulled from the application account.

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: The log object associated with the SailPointContext.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: environment
Argument.Description: Arguments passed to the aggregation task.

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: Application being aggregated.

Argument.name: account
Argument.Description: The resource account for the identity being created.

Argument.name: identity
Argument.Description: The identity that is being created.

Rule.name: AW - BeforeProvisioning Rule
Rule.Description:
An IdentityIQ server-side rule that is executed before the connector's provisioning method is called. This gives the customer the ability to customize or react to anything in the ProvisioningPlan BEFORE it is sent to the underlying connectors used in provisioning.
This rule will be called for any application found in a plan that also has a configured 'beforeProvisioningRule' configured.
The plan can be updated directly in the rule by reference and does not need to return the plan.

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: The log object associated with the SailPointContext.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: plan
Argument.Description: The ProvisioningPlan object on its way to the Connector.

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: The application object that references this before/after script.

Rule.Source: log.error("Provisioning Plan XML: " + plan.toXml());

Argument.Description: The sailpoint.object.Certification to which the item is being added.

Argument.name: certifiable
Argument.Description: The sailpoint.object.Certifiable item for which the item is being created.

Argument.name: certifiableEntity
Argument.Description: The sailpoint.object.AbstractCertifiableEntity from which the certifiable was retrieved.

Argument.name: certContext
Argument.Description: The sailpoint.api.CertificationContext that is being used to build the certification.

Argument.name: entity
Argument.Description: The sailpoint.object.CertificationEntity to be customized.

Identity Correlation Rules are used to find identities to which new accounts can be attached.
A correlation rule must return a Map with one of the specified Return arguments.

Argument.Description: The name of an Identity object.

Argument.name: identity
Argument.Description: A fully resolved Identity object if the rule wants to do its own queries to locate the identity.

Argument.Description: The name of the extended attribute that can be used to locate an existing identity.

Argument.name: identityAttributeValue
Argument.Description: The value of the named extended attribute that can be used to locate an existing identity. This attribute is used together with the identityAttributeName argument.

Rule.name: AW - SSOAuthentication Rule
Rule.Description: A rule used to perform SSO authentication.

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: The log object associated with the SailPointContext.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: httpRequest
Argument.Description: A javax.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest object containing the SSO data.

Argument.name: identity Or Link
Argument.Description: The Identity or Link that has been authenticated via SSO. Starting in 6.1 this rule can return either the Identity or the specific Link that was used to authenticate the user. Returning the Link is the preferred way as it provides the exact account back to the server side which can be helpful when collecting authentication for electronic signatures.

Argument.name: resultMessage
Argument.Description: The message that describes the reason why the validation failed.

Rule.name: AW - WebServiceBeforeOperationRule
Rule.Description: The Web Services connector will call this rule before performing ANY defined operation. This rule can be used to add/update values in the endpoint object before performing the operation and/or add persistent values to the application's data. (See information about what to return for more information)

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: The log object associated with the SailPointContext.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: The application associated with the operation being processed.

Argument.name: requestEndPoint
Argument.Description: The current request information containing the header, body, context url, method type, response attribute map, and response code. This object can be modified directly and returned by the rule to update the endpoint information that is used by the current operation.

Argument.name: oldResponseMap
Argument.Description: Earlier response object

Argument.name: restClient
Argument.Description: REST Client Object

Argument.name: provisioningPlan
Argument.Description: A ProvisioningPlan object containing the payload of the http request. A provisioning plan has an account request which defines the operation to be performed on the account. An account request can contain multiple attribute requests and each attribute request represents an operation on a single account attribute.

Argument.name: partition
Argument.Description: If applicable, a Partition object with the current aggregation's partitioning information.

Argument.name: Map
Argument.Description: An updated or unmodified 'requestEndPoint' object. If application object modifications are desired, create a map containing keys 'updatedEndPoint' and 'connectorStateMap' and use it as the return value; Within the new map, the 'updatedEndPoint' can be set to an updated or unmodified 'requestEndPoint' object. The 'connectorStateMap' will be saved as persistent values in the application definition.

An IdentityIQ server-side rule that is executed before the connector's provisioning method is called. This gives the customer the ability to customize or react to anything in the ProvisioningPlan BEFORE it is sent to the underlying connectors used in provisioning.
This rule will be called for any application found in a plan that also has a configured 'beforeProvisioningRule' configured.
The plan can be updated directly in the rule by reference and does not need to return the plan.

This rule is configured on the application and is called after the connector has build a ResourceObject from the native application data.

Disable Account in Eshiam Web Service Application

Argument.Description: The log object associated with the SailPointContext.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: object
Argument.Description: The ResourceObject built by the connector.

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: Application that references the connector.

Argument.name: connector
Argument.Description: The connector object.

Argument.name: state

Argument.name: resourceObject
Argument.Description: The updated resource object.

Rule.name: AW - AccountSelector Rule
Rule.Description:
AccountSelector Rules are used by the role modeler to automatically select a target account for provisioning when there are multiple candidates.
The return value is a Link instance that represents the selection. Value can be:
null - indicates that no automatic selection can be made
"prompt" - indicates that a separate account selector should be presented for the role
existing Link - the selected Link from the list of possible target Links
a new Link instance - indicates that a new account must be created with the identity specified by the create policy, or manually; in this case, the Link instance returned will have a null nativeIdentity attribute

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: The log object associated with the SailPointContext.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: source
Argument.Description: The application doing the provisioning.

Argument.name: role
Argument.Description: The IT role being provisioned.

Argument.name: identity
Argument.Description: The target identity.

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: The target application.

Argument.name: links
Argument.Description: A list of the possible target Links.

Argument.name: isSecondary
Argument.Description: True if this is not the first assignment.

Argument.name: project
Argument.Description: The provisioning project.

Argument.name: accountRequest
Argument.Description: The account request.

Argument.name: allowCreate
Argument.Description: True if account creation is allowed.

Argument.name: selection
Argument.Description: A Link instance that represents the selection. Value can be: null - indicates that no automatic selection can be made Link - the selected Link from the list of possible target Links a new Link instance - indicates that a new account must be created with the identity specified by the create policy, or manually; in this case, the Link instance returned will have a null nativeIdentity attribute

Rule.Source:
log.error("Entered into AW - AccountSelector Rule");
return null;

Argument.Description: The Identity being inspected.

Argument.name: constraint

Argument.name: violation
Argument.Description: The PolicyViolation object.

Rule.name: ARM Integration Rule Library
ARM Integration Warn Validation Rule DEFAULT
Rule.Description:
Rule to run over access requests to return warn messages to prevent the request from being submitted until they are fixed.
List of validation messages are shown on manage access review page, with each individual message on a new line.
Add bullet points • to the string messages in the list to give a bullet like display of things they need to fix.
Note: At this time, do not throw validation errors for comments/sunrise/sunset on all objects as the validation message may be big for large requests. Just do it on select access.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: requesterName
Argument.Description: String identity name of the user requesting the access.

Argument.name: localeString
Argument.Description:
String representation of the localeString. Not usually needed as the ARV plugin will localize if message keys are passed back.
Method returnLocalizedMessage(validationString, localeString) exists to convert message keys in: import services.tools.plugin.rest.AccessRequestValidationPluginResource;

Argument.name: selectedIdentityNames
Argument.Description: List of strings, where each string is an identity name from identity request.

Argument.name: requestedItems
Argument.Description:
List of maps, each map is an item that was requested to add in the identity request.
DEFINITE Keys with a value in each map are: •id: the id of the item requested •accessType: this will be a string "Entitlement" or "Role"
POSSIBLE Keys with a value in each map are: •comment: the comment on the item the requester has entered •sunsetDate: the sunset date on the item the requester has entered •sunriseDate: the sunrise date on the item the requester has entered
Possible keys won't exist if requester didn't enter any for that.

Argument.name: requestedRemoveItems
Argument.Description:
List of maps, each map is an item that was requested to be removed in the identity request.
DEFINITE Keys with a value in each map are: •id: the id of the item requested •accessType: this will be Entitlement or Role
POSSIBLE Keys with a value in each map are: •comment: the comment on the item the requester has entered •sunsetDate: the sunset date on the item the requester has entered
Possible keys won't exist if requester didn't enter any for that.

Argument.name: validationStrings
Argument.Description:
List or String to return that contains all the validation errors to show to the requesters.
Returning empty list, empty string, or null will not display an error.

Rule.name: AW - CALL_POWERSHELL_RULE
AW - Azure AD Create Mailbox Powershell
Rule.Source:
## Install-Module -Name ExchangeOnlineManagement -Scope AllUsers -Force ## Import-Module ExchangeOnlineManagement -MinimumVersion 3.6.0
$username = "" $pwdText = "" $samAccountName = "" $domain = "yourdomain.onmicrosoft.com"
$secpasswd = ConvertTo-SecureString $pwdText -AsPlainText -Force
$credObject = New-Object System.Management.Automation.PSCredential ($username, $secpasswd)
Connect-ExchangeOnline -Credential $credObject -ShowBanner:$false
New-Mailbox -Shared -Name $samAccountName -DisplayName $samAccountName -Alias $samAccountName -PrimarySmtpAddress "$samAccountName@$domain"
Disconnect-ExchangeOnline -Confirm:$false

Rule.name: AW - Rule_Call_Powershell_Script_to_Create_AD_Account
AW - AD After Create Rule(PowershellScript)
Rule.Description: This example is for IQService Before Script. This is a sample PowerShell after script which ensures that the request was processed successfully and creates home directory at the path specified in the request

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: The log object associated with the SailPointContext.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: plan
Argument.Description: The ProvisioningPlan object on its way to the Connector.

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: The application object that references this before/after script.

Rule.Source:
# Import AD module Import-Module ActiveDirectory
# User details $FirstName = "Test" $LastName = "User" $Username = "Test.User" $Password = ConvertTo-SecureString "P@ssword123!" -AsPlainText -Force $OU = "OU=People,OU=Demo,DC=Eshiam,DC=com" # Change to your actual OU path $UserPrincipalName = "$Username@eshiam.com"
# Create the user New-ADUser ` -Name "$FirstName $LastName" ` -GivenName $FirstName ` -Surname $LastName ` -SamAccountName $Username ` -UserPrincipalName $UserPrincipalName ` -AccountPassword $Password ` -Path $OU ` -Enabled $true ` -ChangePasswordAtLogon $true ` -PasswordNeverExpires $false ` -DisplayName "$FirstName $LastName" ` -EmailAddress $UserPrincipalName ` -Description "Created via PowerShell script"
Write-Host "User $Username created successfully."

Argument.Description: A map where the key is a string and value is a string. The map will always contain a key NameId with the value being the name Id sent by the Identity Provider. All other SAML assertion attributes will be included in the map.

Argument.name: identity Or Link
Argument.Description: The Identity or Link that has been authenticated via SAML SSO. Returning the Link is the preferred way as it provides the exact account back to the server side which can be helpful when collecting authentication for electronic signatures.

Objects in Requestor's Authorized Scopes
Rule.Description: This rule returns either a Filter object that will be used to search for objects in the requestor's authorized scopes, the boolean value 'true' to indicate that everything should be returned (i.e. no restrictions) or the boolean value 'false' to indicate that no objects are available for this user

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: The log object associated with the SailPointContext.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: requestor
Argument.Description: Identity that is making the Life Cycle Manager request.

Argument.name: requestee
Argument.Description: Identity on whose behalf the Life Cycle Manager request is being made. In the case of bulk requests, this argument will be set to null when determining the roles that are visible to the requestor. It will be provided once a selection has been made in order to determine whether or not the given requestee should have access to the selected role.

Argument.name: filter
Argument.Description: Either a Filter object that will be used to search for accessible Objects, the boolean value 'true' to indicate that everything should be returned (i.e. no restrictions) or the boolean value 'false' to indicate that no objects are available for this population

Rule.name: Objects in Requestee's Assigned Scope
Rule.Description: This rule returns a Filter that restricts objects to those in the requestee's assigned scope

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: The log object associated with the SailPointContext.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: requestor
Argument.Description: Identity that is making the Life Cycle Manager request.

Argument.name: requestee
Argument.Description: Identity on whose behalf the Life Cycle Manager request is being made. In the case of bulk requests, this argument will be set to null when determining the objects that are visible to the requestor. It will be provided once a selection has been made in order to determine whether or not the given requestee should have access to the selected role.

Argument.name: filter
Argument.Description: Either a Filter object that will be used to search for accessible Objects, the boolean value 'true' to indicate that everything should be returned (i.e. no restrictions) or the boolean value 'false' to indicate that no objects are available for this population

Rule.name: Objects in Requestor's Authorized Scopes or Requestee's Assigned Scope
Rule.Description: This rule returns a Filter that restricts roles to those in the requestor's authorized scopes or the requestee's assigned scope

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: The log object associated with the SailPointContext.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: requestor
Argument.Description: Identity that is making the Life Cycle Manager request.

Argument.name: requestee
Argument.Description: Identity on whose behalf the Life Cycle Manager request is being made. In the case of bulk requests, this argument will be set to null when determining the roles that are visible to the requestor. It will be provided once a selection has been made in order to determine whether or not the given requestee should have access to the selected role.

Argument.name: filter
Argument.Description: A Filter object that will be used to search for accessible objects.

Rule.name: Objects Owned by the Requestor
Rule.Description: This rule returns a Filter that restricts objects to those that the requestor or one of their workgroups owns

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: The log object associated with the SailPointContext.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: requestor
Argument.Description: Identity that is making the Life Cycle Manager request.

Argument.name: requestee
Argument.Description: Identity on whose behalf the Life Cycle Manager request is being made. In the case of bulk requests, this argument will be set to null when determining the objects that are visible to the requestor. It will be provided once a selection has been made in order to determine whether or not the given requestee should have access to the selected object.

Argument.name: isUnmatchedEntitlements
Argument.Description: A boolean flag which shows that we need a filter for entitlements without matched ManagedAttributes if true. To filter such entitlements the MapMatcher is used. MapMatcher does not support subquery, that's why a filter with "in" instead of subquery should be created.

Argument.name: isCurrentAccess
Argument.Description: A boolean flag which shows that we select user's current access if true.

Argument.name: filter
Argument.Description: A Filter object that will be used to search for accessible Objects.

Rule.name: All Objects
Rule.Description: Allows all objects to be selected (no filtering)

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: The log object associated with the SailPointContext.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: requestor
Argument.Description: Identity that is making the Life Cycle Manager request.

Argument.name: requestee
Argument.Description: Identity on whose behalf the Life Cycle Manager request is being made. In the case of bulk requests, this argument will be set to null when determining the roles that are visible to the requestor. It will be provided once a selection has been made in order to determine whether or not the given requestee should have access to the selected role.

Argument.name: filter
Argument.Description: A Filter object that will be used to search for accessible request objects.

Rule.name: CEFActivityCorrelation
Rule.Description: Rule to show how an CEF activity might be correlated back to an identity. In most cases, this rule will be providing enought information to the correlator so that it can find the Application link. In some cases, it might also be desireable to return something that points back to an Identity, which is also supported.

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: The log object associated with the SailPointContext.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: The application where the activity was generated. An application may have more then one data source.

Argument.name: datasource
Argument.Description: The data source that returned the activity.

Argument.name: activity
Argument.Description: The activity object that was normalized by the data source and that we are trying to correlate back to one of our Identities.

Argument.name: linkIdentity
Argument.Description: A string that represents the link's identity attribute.

Argument.name: linkName
Argument.Description: A string that represents the link's displayName attribute.

Argument.name: linkAttributeName
Argument.Description: A string that represents the NAME of an attribute that can be used to find the Link that maps back to this activity. This attribute must also be supplied with the linkAttributeValue variable.

Argument.name: linkAttributeValue
Argument.Description: A string that represents the VALUE of an attribute that can be used to find the Link that maps back to this activity. This attribute must also be supplied with the linkAttributeName variable.

Argument.name: identityName
Argument.Description: The name of an Identity object.

Argument.name: identity
Argument.Description: A fully resolved Identity object in case the rule wants to do its own queries to locate the identity.

Argument.name: identityAttributeName
Argument.Description: The name of the extended attribute that can be used to locate an existing identity.

Argument.name: identityAttributeValue
Argument.Description: The value of the named extended attribute that can be used to locate an existing identity. This attribute is used together with the identityAttributeName argument.

Rule.name: Application Builder
Rule.Description: A rule to build and manipulate IdentityIQ application in a bulk. All duties are mentioned below. 1. When create mode is selected, then it creates multiple application in a bulk by parsing csv file. 2. When update mode is selected, then it updates the attribute map of existing application by parsing input csv file. 3. When read mode is selected, then it reads all existing IdentityIQ application and export them to csv file. Exports considers only attributes of the application.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database to save application object and task definitions.

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: The log object for logging.

Argument.name: inputParams
Argument.Description: The input params object is map of attributes received from Task UI.

Argument.name: taskResult
Argument.Description: The task result object required for updating the result.

Rule.name: Azure AD Create Mailbox Powershell - New
Rule.Source:
# Load the SailPoint Utils DLL Add-Type -Path "C:\SailPoint\IQService\Utils.dll"
# Log file path $logFile = "C:\SailPoint\mailbox.log"
# Function to write to log function Write-ToLog { param([string]$message) $timestamp = Get-Date -Format "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss" Add-Content -Path $logFile -Value "$timestamp :: $message" }
Write-ToLog "PowerShell script started."

Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: result
Argument.Description: A boolean describing the result of the rule.

Rule.name: BNY_Approval_Assignment_Rule-SR
Rule.Description:
This example rule switches all of the owners to spadmin. The idea of this rule is to allow some customization of the approvals so the ownership can be calculated for cases where you might use and extended attribute or some other means outside our default mechanisms to derive the owner of the Approval.
This rule is typically configured on the Approval Step in the LCM Provisioning Workflow or can be set on any steps based on the "Provisioning Approval" step library.
Since 6.2

Argument.Description: The log object associated with the SailPointContext.
A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.
List of approvals generated by the approvalScheme, maybe null if there were no approvals enabled.
Representation of the cart, also found on the approvals generated by the default schemes.
Return a lIst of Workflow.Approval objects that should be included during the approval process.

Rule.name: Example Manager Correlation Rule
Rule.Description:
A rule called to help IdentityIQ take a value from the mapped manager field and map it to an existing IdentityIQ identity.
Just like identity correlation, the rule needs to return a map that contains some values that will guide the correlator on how to try to find the identity. It can also do the lookup manually and just returned the identity.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object

Argument.name: environment
Argument.Description: Arguments passed to the aggregation task.

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: The application the aggregated account is from.

Argument.name: instance
Argument.Description: Optional instance identifier within the application.

Argument.name: connector
Argument.Description: A connector to the attribute source.

Argument.name: link
Argument.Description: Account link of the referencing identity.

Argument.name: managerAttributeValue
Argument.Description: Attribute value that is stored in the manager attribute.

Argument.Description: The name of an Identity object.

Argument.name: identity
Argument.Description: A fully resolved Identity object if the rule wants to do its own queries to locate the identity.

Argument.name: identityAttributeName
Argument.Description: The name of the extended attribute that can be used to locate an existing identity.

Argument.name: identityAttributeValue
Argument.Description: The value of the named extended attribute that can be used to locate an existing identity. This attribute is used together with the identityAttributeName argument.

Rule.name: Exclude Inactive Identities
Rule.Description: This rule is an example Certification Exclusion rule that removes all of the certifiable items from a certification if the identity being certified is marked as inactive.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the system or execute other SailPointContext services.

Argument.name: entity
Argument.Description: The AbstractCertifiableEntity that is part of the certification. Currently, this is either an Identity, AccountGroup, or Bundle.

Argument.name: certification
Argument.Description: The certification that this identity is part of.

Argument.name: certContext
Argument.Description: The CertificationContext that is being used to generate the certification.

Argument.name: items
Argument.Description: List of Certifiable items that are currently part of the certification for this identity. Any items that should be excluded from the certification should be deleted from this list and added to the itemsToExclude list.

Argument.name: itemsToExclude
Argument.Description: A List of Certifiable items that should not be included in the certification. This list will be empty when the rule is executed and any items that should not be part of the certification should be moved from the items list to the itemsToExclude list.

Argument.name: explanation
Argument.Description: An optional explanation describing why the items were excluded.

Rule.name: Exclude Composite Accounts
Rule.Description: This rule splits the certifiables in the items and itemsToExclude list based on whether the certifiable is an "Exception" on an account that is a part of a composite account. If we determine that the account is part of a composite account, the item is removed from the items list and added to the itemsToExclude list.

Rule.name: Native Identity Composite Account Rule
Rule.Description: This is an account rule used by the RuleCompositeConnector that creates composite links if the given Identity has Links for all applications in the "tiers" resource attribute, and all of these links share the same native identity.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A SailPointContext object used if its necessary to query objects from the database.

Argument.name: identity
Argument.Description: The Identity currently being processed.

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: The composite application.

Argument.name: compositeLink
Argument.Description: A single composite Link or a Collection of composite Links.

Rule.name: Example Work Item Forward to Valid Email
Rule.Description: This example checks the Identity that owns the work item for an email address. If there's no email address for the Identity, the rule trolls up the management chain until it finds a manager with an email, then assigns that person to be the owner of the work item.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the system or execute other SailPointContext services.

Argument.name: item
Argument.Description: The WorkItem being opened or forwarded.

Argument.name: owner
Argument.Description: The Identity that currently owns the work item.

Argument.name: newOwner
Argument.Description: An Identity that should own the work item. Alternatively, this can be a String that is the name or id of the Identity.

Rule.name: Example Group Owner Assignment
Rule.Description: A rule used to assign owners to groups generated from a group factory.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the system or execute other SailPointContext services.

Argument.name: factory
Argument.Description: The GroupFactory that generated the populations.

Argument.name: group
Argument.Description: One GroupDefinition from the factory.

Argument.name: owner
Argument.Description: The group owner.

Rule.Source: return "spadmin";

Rule.name: Example Certifier Assignment
Rule.Description: A rule used to assign certifiers to populations generated from a group factory.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the system or execute other SailPointContext services.

Argument.name: factory
Argument.Description: The GroupFactory that generated the populations.

Argument.name: group
Argument.Description: One GroupDefinition from the factory.

Argument.name: certifiers
Argument.Description: A String, List<String>, Identity, or a List<Identity>. When returning strings, the string must be the name or id of an Identity. When returning a single string, the string may be formatted as a CSV of Identity names or ids.

Rule.Source: return "spadmin";

Rule.name: Example Targeted Certification Primary Certifier
Rule.Description: A rule used to assign certifiers to entities in a Targeted certification. In this example, we check if the identity referenced by the entity is privileged, in which case we send to a workgroup named "PrivelegedAdminWorkgroup". If not privileged, send the manager if exists, lastly fallback to spadmin.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the system or execute other SailPointContext services.

Argument.name: certification
Argument.Description: The root sailpoint.object.Certification being created. At the point this rule is run, there wont be any entities or items in this certtification yet, it is a stub that is used for various things while we are partitioning work to construct entities and assign them to other certifications in the campaign.

Argument.name: definition
Argument.Description: The sailpoint.object.CertificationDefinition for the certification.

Argument.name: entity
Argument.Description: The sailpoint.object.CertificationEntity to assign a certifier to

Argument.name: state
Argument.Description: A map object that can be used to store data during the iteration process.

Argument.name: certifiers
Argument.Description: A String or List<String> When returning strings, the string must be the name of an Identity. A single string is for a single certifier, or a List of Strings can be multiple certifiers.

Rule.name: Example Certification Pre-delegation Rule
Rule.Description: A pre-delegation rule is run on every CertificationEntity when the certification is generated in order to cause some entities to be pre-delegated. As an example, a manager certification could pre-delegate the certification responsibility to each employee that reports to the manager. This would allow each subordinate to first determine what they need access to, then allow the manager to review their decision.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: The SailPointContext to use.

Argument.name: certification
Argument.Description: The sailpoint.object.Certification being created.

Argument.name: entity
Argument.Description: The sailpoint.object.CertificationEntity to possibly pre-delegate.

Argument.name: certContext
Argument.Description: The sailpoint.api.CertificationContext generating this certification.

Argument.name: recipientName
Argument.Description: The name of the Identity that should certify this entity. Either this or 'recipient' should be non-null if pre-delegation should be performed.

Argument.name: recipient
Argument.Description: The Identity that should certify this entity. Either this or 'recipientName' should be non-null if pre-delegation should be performed.

Argument.name: description
Argument.Description: Optional description to set on the delegation WorkItem. If null, a default description of "Certify [entity name]" is used.

Argument.name: comments
Argument.Description: Optional comments to set on the delegation WorkItems.

Argument.name: reassign
Argument.Description: Optional boolean to specify to reassign rather than delegate.

Argument.name: certificationName
Argument.Description: Optional String to specify the name for the reassignment certification if creating a new certification for reassignment. This is ignored for delegations.

Rule.name: Example Certification Entity Customization Rule
Rule.Description: A CertificationEntity customization rule is run when a certification is generated to allow the CertificationEntity to be customized. For example, default values can be calculated for the custom fields.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext to use.

Argument.name: certification
Argument.Description: The sailpoint.object.Certification to which the item is being added.

Argument.name: certifiable
Argument.Description: The sailpoint.object.Certifiable item for which the item is being created.

Argument.name: certifiableEntity
Argument.Description: The sailpoint.object.AbstractCertifiableEntity from which the certifiable was retrieved.

Argument.name: certContext
Argument.Description: The sailpoint.api.CertificationContext that is being used to build the certification.

Argument.name: entity
Argument.Description: The sailpoint.object.CertificationEntity to be customized.

Rule.name: Example Certification Entity Completion Rule
Rule.Description: A CertificationEntity completion rule is run when a CertificationEntity is refreshed and has been determined to be otherwise complete (ie - all items on the entity are complete). This rule determines whether the entity is still missing any information. For example, the entity may require a "classification" value to be present in a custom field to be complete. If errors are found, the error messages (either plain-text messages or keys that map to messages in the custom message catalog) are added to a List and returned to the caller.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A SailPointContext object used if its necessary to query objects from the database.

Argument.name: certification
Argument.Description: The sailpoint.object.Certification being refreshed.

Argument.name: entity
Argument.Description: The sailpoint.object.CertificationEntity being refreshed.

Argument.name: messages
Argument.Description: A List of Message objects if errors were found; null otherwise.

Rule.name: Example Certification Entity Refresh Rule
Rule.Description: A CertificationEntity refresh rule is run when a CertificationEntity is refreshed. For example, this can copy a custom field from the CertificationEntity down onto all sub-items.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A SailPointContext object used if its necessary to query objects from the database.

Argument.name: certification
Argument.Description: The sailpoint.object.Certification being refreshed.

Argument.name: entity
Argument.Description: The sailpoint.object.CertificationEntity being refreshed.

Rule.name: Example Certification Schedule Entity Selector by Filter Rule
Rule.Description: This rule is optionally run to determine which entities to certify as part of a certification campaign. It can return either a Filter object or a list of identity names. In this example, the rule returns a Filter that will be used to find the identities to include as entities, by including those who either are managers or have no manager.

Argument.name: definition
Argument.Description: The sailpoint.object.CertificationDefinition that is being used to generate the certification campaign

Argument.name: Filter or List of Strings
Argument.Description: If returning a Filter, that will be used to query for sailpoint.object.Identity names to include. If returning a list of strings, those should be the names of sailpoint.object.Identity objects to include.

Rule.name: Example Certification Schedule Entity Selector by Name Rule
Rule.Description: This rule is optionally run to determine which entities to certify as part of a certification campaign. It can return either a Filter object or a list of identity names. In this example, the rule searches for inactive identities with privileged accounts and returns their names.

Argument.name: definition
Argument.Description: The sailpoint.object.CertificationDefinition that is being used to generate the certification campaign

Argument.name: Filter or List of Strings
Argument.Description: If returning a Filter, that will be used to query for sailpoint.object.Identity names to include. If returning a list of strings, those should be the names of sailpoint.object.Identity objects to include.

Rule.name: Example Certification Sign Off Approver Rule
Rule.Description: This rule is run when the certification is signed off to determine who (if anyone) needs to approve the certification decisions. If this returns a non-null identity, the certification is forwarded to the returned identity.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A SailPointContext object used if its necessary to query objects from the database.

Argument.name: certification
Argument.Description: The sailpoint.object.Certification that was signed.

Argument.name: certifier
Argument.Description: The sailpoint.object.Identity that signed the certification.

Argument.name: identityName
Argument.Description: The name of the Identity that should approve the certification. Either this or 'identity' should be non-null if certification sign off approval is desired.

Argument.name: identity
Argument.Description: The Identity that should approve the certification. Either this or 'identityName' should be non-null if certification sign off approval is desired.

Rule.name: Example Certification Event Rule
Rule.Description: This rule can be used for Certification Events that use rule triggers. The previousIdentity and newIdentity hold a snapshot of the identity before the update has occurred and the current state of the identity (after the update), respectively. In this example, we create a new certification for the identity if the composite risk score goes over a threshold of 700.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext.

Argument.name: previousIdentity
Argument.Description: The identity before the refresh/aggregation (this will be null when an identity is created).

Argument.name: newIdentity
Argument.Description: The identity after the refresh/aggregation (this will be null when an identity is deleted).

Rule.name: Example Scope Correlation Rule
Rule.Description: Given an identity and the defined scope correlation attribute and value, determine the correct assigned scope for the Identity. This can return a single Scope (if we can correlate to a distinct scope), a List of Scopes (if we can't determine a distinct scope), or null (if there is not an assigned scope for the given identity).

Argument.name: identity
Argument.Description: The identity for which to correlate the assigned scope.

Argument.name: scopeCorrelationAttribute
Argument.Description: The name of the scope correlation attribute on the identity.

Argument.name: scopeCorrelationAttributeValue
Argument.Description: The value of the scope correlation attribute on the given identity.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database to aid in correlation.

Argument.name: scopes
Argument.Description: Either a Scope or a List that contains multiple scopes that could correlate to the given identity.

Rule.name: Example Scope Selection Rule
Rule.Description: This rule is run if scope correlation for an identity's assigned scope (either from a scope correlation attribute or rule) returns a list of scopes because the correlation is ambiguous. This can happen since scopes are not guaranteed to have unique names. This rule is responsible for selecting the correct scope out of the candidateScopes list for the given identity. If the correct scope cannot be determined, this rule should return null.

Argument.name: identity
Argument.Description: The identity for which to correlate the assigned scope.

Argument.name: scopeCorrelationAttribute
Argument.Description: The name of the scope correlation attribute on the identity.

Argument.name: scopeCorrelationAttributeValue
Argument.Description: The value of the scope correlation attribute on the given identity.

Argument.name: candidateScopes
Argument.Description: The List of scopes that were correlated to the given Identity. A single Scope from this list should be selected and returned if it is possible to determine the correct scope.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database to aid in selection.

Argument.name: scope
Argument.Description: A single scope selected from the candidateScopes List, or null if it is impossible to determine the correct scope for the given identity.

Rule.name: Example PreIterate Rule
Rule.Description: An example Preiterate rule for the DelimitedFileConnector that can check stats of the file being processed against stats collected and persisted during the last aggregation in the PostIterate rule.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A SailPointContext object used if its necessary to query objects from the database.

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: Application being iterated.

Argument.name: schema
Argument.Description: Schema representing the data being iterated

Argument.name: stats
Argument.Description: A map passed by the connector of the stats for the file about to be iterated. Contains keys: fileName : (String) filename of the file about to be processed absolutePath : (String) absolute filename length : (Long) length in bytes lastModified : (Long) last time the file was updated Java GMT

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: log for debugging

Rule.name: Example PostIterate Rule
Rule.Description:
An example PostIterate rule that can be configured on a DelimitedFileConnector.
This rule add stats to a Configuration object and persists it with a well-known name that will be checked by the PreIterate rule during the next run for file integrity.
The Configuration object created by this rule contains a key for each schema type so we can have a single object for each application.
Stats is a Map passed in by the connector with the information it has gathered. Custom keys can be added here if necessary for for validation.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A SailPointContext object used if its necessary to query objects from the database.

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: log for debugging

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: Application being iterated

Argument.name: schema
Argument.Description: Schema requested during iteration

Argument.name: stats
Argument.Description: A map of the stats for the current file that was just iterated. Contains keys: fileName : (String) filename of the file about to be processed absolutePath : (String) absolute filename length : (Long) length in bytes lastModified : (Long) last time the file was updated Java GMT columnNames : (List) column names that were used during the iteration objectsIterated : (Long) total number of objects iterated during this run

Rule.name: Example JDBC Provisioning Rule
Rule.Description: An example Provisioning rule for the JDBC Connector that deletes the Account(s) from the database.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database to aid in provisioning.

Argument.name: application

Argument.name: schema

Argument.name: connection
Argument.Description: Connection to the application database.

Argument.name: plan
Argument.Description: The ProvisioningPlan created against the application.

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: log for debugging

Rule.Description: An example Provisioning rule for the JDBC Connector that enables the Account(s) in the database.

Argument.name: schema

Argument.name: plan
Argument.Description: The ProvisioningPlan created against the application.

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: log for debugging

Argument.name: request
Argument.Description: request for specific operation

Rule.Description: An example Provisioning rule for the JDBC Connector that deletes the Account(s) from the database.

Argument.name: application

Rule.Description: An example Provisioning rule for the JDBC Connector that unlocks the Account(s) in the database.

Rule.Description: An example Provisioning rule for the JDBC Connector that creates the Account(s) in the database.

Argument.name: schema

Rule.name: JDBC Modify Provision Rule
Rule.Description: An example Provisioning rule for the JDBC Connector that modify the Account(s) in the database.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database to aid in provisioning.

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: The Application the account is being provisioned for.

Argument.name: schema
Argument.Description: Schema representing the data being provisioned.

Argument.name: connection

Argument.Description: log for debugging

Argument.name: request
Argument.Description: request for specific operation

Rule.name: Render SOD Entitlements
Rule.Description:
An example rendering rule that will generate a multi-line string containing details of the entitlements involved in a polcy violation. This may be used for both Role SOD policies and Entitlement SOD policies.
sailpoint.api.PolicyUtil does most of the work, we render the resulting EntitlementSummary.
EntitlementSummary is an object with two lists one for the "left" side and one for the "right". Due to space limitations in description table cells we'll render the left on top and right on the bottom seperated by a line of text "--- conflicts with ---".

Rule.name: Example Composite Remediation Rule
Rule.Description:
A example rule that is called when provisioning is about to be performed against a composite connector. The rule needs to convert the provisioning plan built up for the composite connector into a plan that will target the applications that make up the composite application.
In this example we have a composite rule for an application named FinanceDemo that is made up of two "tiers" a db application named OracleDB and an LDAP application named CorpDirectory.
The entitlements of the composite accounts all come from the "groups" attribute on the CorpDirectory application which maps to the "groupMember" attribute on the FinanceDemo application.
This rule will look through the AccountRequest for the FinanceDemo application sifting through the attribute requests looking for requests that target the groupMember attribute covert them to target the groups attribute on the CorpDirectory application.

Argument.name: identity
Argument.Description: The Identity object that is going to be remediated.

Argument.name: plan
Argument.Description: The ProvisioningPlan created against the composite application.

Argument.name: provisioningPlan
Argument.Description: A converted provisioning plan that targets the applications that make up the composite application.

Rule.name: Example Identity Display Name Generation Rule
Rule.Description: Example rule that can be used to generate an identity's displayName during attribute promotion.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A SailPointContext object used if its necessary to query objects from the database.

Argument.name: environment
Argument.Description: Arguments passed to the aggregation or refresh task.

Argument.name: identity
Argument.Description: The Identity object that represents the user that is being aggregated.

Argument.name: attributeDefinition
Argument.Description: The AttributeDefinition object for this attribute.

Argument.name: attributeValue
Argument.Description: The value of the attribute that should be populated. The rule should return this value.

Rule.name: Example Create Account Name
Rule.Description:
Example rule that can be used to generate an account name for an identity when creating a new account.
Attempt to use the fullname. If it's null, then use the identity's name, which is guaranteed to be non-null.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A SailPointContext object used if its necessary to query objects from the database.

Argument.name: identity
Argument.Description: The Identity object that represents the user that the account is being created for.

Argument.name: accountRequest
Argument.Description: The account request within the provisioning plan that is being compiled.

Argument.name: field
Argument.Description: The field within the provisioning policy that is being processed.

Argument.name: [field values]
Argument.Description: This is not the name of an actual argument. It means that the values of all fields that appear before this field will be included as arguments. The names of the arguments will be the names of the previous fields.

Argument.name: [workflow arguments]
Argument.Description: This is not the name of an actual argument. It means that when the field is being processed within a workflow, the values of the step arguments will be passed as arguments to any rules or scripts evaluated within the step.

Argument.name: accountName
Argument.Description: The string value of the new account name (first initial and last name).

Rule.name: Policy Violation Owner Rule
Rule.Description: An example rule that can be called to assign a default owner for a policy violation.

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: The log object associated with the SailPointContext.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: identity
Argument.Description: The identity.

Argument.name: roleName
Argument.Description: The name of the sailpoint.object.Bundle (role) that is being selected for the Identity. If roles are not applicable to this Identity selection, this value will be void.

Argument.name: success
Argument.Description: True if the selection was successful; false otherwise.

Rule.name: Example Email Recipient Rule
Rule.Description: This rule is used to select an identity or a list of identities who will receive email. In this case the manager of work item owner is selected to be notified.

Argument.name: item
Argument.Description: The sailpoint.object.Notifiable Interface for objects that can be reminded, escalated, and expired.

Argument.name: identity name or names
Argument.Description: You can return an identity name or a list of identity names.

Rule.Source: return item.getOwner().getManager().getName();

Rule.name: Example SAP BuildMap calling a BAPI Function Module
Rule.Description:
This is an example of a buildMap rule for SAP that uses a BAPI call to add additional information to the ResourceObjects built by the default SAP Connector implementation.
The rule gets a Map named 'object' that holds all of the attributes we've built up using the default SAP implementation. The object can be modified/added to here to help support customer customizations and transformations that require the SAP system.
In this example rule we call the BAPI_USER_GET_DETAIL function module and adorn the LASTMODIFIED attributes to our object because they are not part of the base implementation.

Argument.name: destination
Argument.Description: A connected/ready to use com.sap.conn.jco.JCoDestination object that can be used to call bapi, function modules and call to SAP tables. This is the main object used in making BAPI calls using the JCo interface. This destination object is shared with the connector implementation and the connector controls the destination's lifecycle.

Argument.name: schema
Argument.Description: The schema that represents the object we are building.

Argument.name: connector
Argument.Description: The SAP connector that is being used to communicate with SAP. This class is here for convenience as there are many utility methods that make calling Function Modules and doing table lookup easier

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: The SAP application object being used to connect to the SAP system.

Argument.name: state
Argument.Description: A map object that can be used to store data during the iteration process.

Argument.name: object
Argument.Description: A map object that holds the attributes that have been built up by the default connector implementation. Any attributes built by this rule should be added to this object. This object is handed by reference, so it can be used directly.

Rule.name: Example SAP BuildMap calling a BAPI to Query an SAP table
Rule.Description:
This is an example of a buildMap rule for SAP that uses a BAPI call to add additional information to the ResourceObjects built by the default SAP Connector implementation.
The rule gets a Map named 'object' that holds all of the attributes we've built up using the default SAP implementation. The object can be modified/added to here to help support customer customizations and transformations that require the SAP system.
In this example rule we call to the ARG_TEXTS table to get the long description for a role object during group aggregation. By default the connector puts this value in a multi-valued "Long Description" field, but here we concatenate all of the lines together into a single value and put it in the Description field.

Argument.name: destination
Argument.Description: A connected/ready to use com.sap.conn.jco.JCoDestination object that can be used to call bapi, function modules and call to SAP tables. This is the main object used in making BAPI calls using the JCo interface. This destination object is shared with the connector implementation and the connector controls the destination's lifecycle.

Argument.name: schema
Argument.Description: The schema that represents the object we are building.

Argument.name: connector
Argument.Description: The SAP connector that is being used to communicate with SAP. This class is here for convenience as there are many utility methods that make calling Function Modules and doing table lookup easier

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: The SAP application object being used to connect to the SAP system.

Argument.name: state
Argument.Description: A map object that can be used to store data during the iteration process.

Argument.name: object
Argument.Description: A map object that holds the attributes that have been built up by the default connector implementation. Any attributes built by this rule should be added to this object. This object is handed by reference, so it can be used directly.

Rule.name: Example SAP HRMS Modify Rule
Rule.Description: This rule is used by the SAP HR connector for provisioning of the data .

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: The log object associated with the SailPointContext.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: The application being processed.

Argument.name: schema
Argument.Description: The Schema currently in use.

Argument.name: destination
Argument.Description: A connected/ready to use com.sap.conn.jco.JCoDestination object that can be used to call BAPI, function modules and call to SAP tables. This is the main object used in making BAPI calls using the JCo interface.This destination object is shared with the connector implementation and the connector controls the destination's lifecycle.

Argument.name: plan
Argument.Description: The ProvisioningPlan created against the SAP application.

Argument.name: request
Argument.Description: The ProvisioningRequest created against the SAP application.

Argument.name: connector
Argument.Description: The SAP connector that is being used to communicate with SAP. This class is here for convenience as there are many utility methods that make calling Function Modules and doing table lookup easier.

Argument.name: result
Argument.Description: A Provisioning Result object is desirable to return the status.IT can be a new object or part of Provisioning Plan

Rule.name: Example Rule For Modifying Attributes In SuccessFactors
Rule.Description: This rule is used by the SuccessFactors connector to do provisioning of username,business email and business phone number.

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: The application whose data file is being processed.

Argument.name: schema
Argument.Description: The Schema currently in use.

Argument.name: plan
Argument.Description: The ProvisioningPlan created against the SuccessFactors application.

Argument.name: request
Argument.Description: The ProvisioningRequest created against the SuccessFactors application.

Argument.name: connector
Argument.Description: Connector object created against the SuccessFactors application.

Argument.name: provisionMap
Argument.Description: ProvisionMap object created against SuccessFactors application which holds information for business phone, business email and username.

Argument.name: successFactorsRestSession
Argument.Description: A connected/ready to use successFactorsRestSession object which can be used for making calls to SuccessFactors ODATA API. This object already has the valid Bearer token.

Argument.name: result
Argument.Description: A Provisioning Result object is desirable to return the status.IT can be a new object or part of Provisioning Plan

Rule.name: Example Managed Attribute Promotion Rule
Rule.Description:
A ManagedAttributePromotion rule is called when ManagedAttributes are automatically created during aggregation, refresh, or from the missing entitlement descriptions task.
This can modify the ManagedAttribute that is passed in to set fields such as owner, requestable, or explanations before they are saved.

Argument.name: attribute
Argument.Description: The sailpoint.object.ManagedAttribute being promoted.

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: The sailpoint.object.Application the managed attribute is from.

Argument.name: state
Argument.Description: A Map that contains managed attribute customization shared state during a task run.

Rule.name: Example SAP GRC Application Role Query
Rule.Description:
This is an example of a buildMap rule for SAP that uses a BAPI call to add additional information to the ResourceObjects built by the default SAP Connector implementation.
The rule gets a Map named 'object' that holds all of the attributes we've built up using the default SAP implementation. The object can be modified/added to here to help support customer customizations and transformations that require the SAP system.
In this example rule we call to table HRP1852 to get the GRC Application Roles assigned to the backend SAP account.

Argument.name: destination
Argument.Description: A connected/ready to use com.sap.conn.jco.JCoDestination object that can be used to call bapi, function modules and call to SAP tables. This is the main object used in making BAPI calls using the JCo interface. This destination object is shared with the connector implementation and the connector controls the destination's lifecycle.

Argument.name: schema
Argument.Description: The schema that represents the object we are building.

Argument.name: connector
Argument.Description: The SAP connector that is being used to communicate with SAP. This class is here for convenience as there are many utility methods that make calling Function Modules and doing table lookup easier

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: The SAP application object being used to connect to the SAP system.

Argument.name: state
Argument.Description: A map object that can be used to store data during the iteration process.

Argument.name: object
Argument.Description: A map object that holds the attributes that have been built up by the default connector implementation. Any attributes built by this rule should be added to this object. This object is handed by reference, so it can be used directly.

Rule.name: Sign Off on End Phase
Rule.Description: When end phase is reached it marks all incomplete items as revoked and signs off on the cer.

Argument.name: certification
Argument.Description: The sailpoint.object.Certification being certified.

Argument.name: certificationItem
Argument.Description: The sailpoint.object.CertificationItem that is being certified.

Argument.name: previousPhase
Argument.Description: Certification.Phase (may be null) that is the previous phase.

Argument.name: nextPhase
Argument.Description: Certification.Phase (may be null) that is the next phase.

Rule.name: Roles in the Requestor's Department
Rule.Description: This rule returns a Filter that restricts roles to those in the requestor's department

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: The log object associated with the SailPointContext.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: requestor
Argument.Description: Identity that is making the Life Cycle Manager request.

Argument.name: requestee
Argument.Description: Identity on whose behalf the Life Cycle Manager request is being made. In the case of bulk requests, this argument will be set to null when determining the roles that are visible to the requestor. It will be provided once a selection has been made in order to determine whether or not the given requestee should have access to the selected role.

Argument.name: filter
Argument.Description: A Filter object that will be used to search for accessible Roles.

Rule.name: Example Allowed Values Rule
Rule.Description: Allowed Values Rules are used by provisioning policies to determine the possible values of the policy fields. Note that an Attributes map of all arguments from the current WorkflowContext is passed into the Allowed Values Rule, so any workflow context arguments are available within this rule.

Argument.name: identity
Argument.Description: The Identity object being provisioned.

Argument.name: form
Argument.Description: The Form object for the provisioning plan.

Argument.name: field
Argument.Description: The Field object being analyzed.

Argument.name: value
Argument.Description: An Obect (possibly a Collection) of the allowed values for the given field.

Rule.name: Example Validation Rule
Rule.Description: This rule is used to validate the Field values on a provisioning plan.

Argument.name: identity
Argument.Description: The Identity object being provisioned.

Argument.name: app
Argument.Description: The Application object being processed.

Argument.name: form
Argument.Description: The Form object being processed.

Argument.name: field
Argument.Description: The Field object being validated.

Argument.name: value
Argument.Description: The Object representing the field value.

Argument.name: value
Argument.Description: An Obect (possibly a Collection) of messages resulting from the validation rule.

Rule.name: Example Owner Rule
Rule.Description: Owner Rules are used by provisioning policies to determine the owner of the provisioning policy or its policy fields.

Argument.name: identity
Argument.Description: The Identity object being provisioned.

Argument.name: role
Argument.Description: The Role object involved in the provisioning processed.

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: The Application object being provisioned.

Argument.name: template
Argument.Description: The Template object that defines the provisioning plan.

Argument.name: field
Argument.Description: The Field object being processed.

Argument.name: identity
Argument.Description: Either the Identity of the owner, or the type of template used to define the provisioning policy, from which the owner can be determined.

Rule.name: Example Workflow Approval Owner Rule
Rule.Description: Workflow rules can be called in a variety of places within the workflow to do a number of different things. This one uses a WorkflowContext variable called "identityName" to find the identity's manager and return the manager's name as the approval owner.

Argument.name: wfcontext
Argument.Description: The current WorkflowContext.

Argument.name: handler
Argument.Description: The workflow handler associated with the current WorkflowContext.

Argument.name: workflow
Argument.Description: The current Workflow definition.

Argument.name: step
Argument.Description: The current Step.

Argument.name: approval
Argument.Description: The current Approval.

Argument.name: item
Argument.Description: The WorkItem being processed.

Argument.name: Object
Argument.Description: The result of the workflow rule; dependent on the rule itself.

Rule.name: Example Domino Group Member Customization Rule
Rule.Description:
This example can be used to derive a group attribute that reflects only group objects. Its specifically designed with Domino in mind, but only uses the attribute name from Domino and can easliy be changed.
The underlying issue is that Domino's member list for groups includes both user's and group objects. To use IIQs group hierarchy the value needs to include only other group object dn's.
This doesn't come for free, and requires us to query back to ldap to try and bind to an object using the connector. If the bind throws ObjectNotFoundException we can assume the object type.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A SailPointContext object used if its necessary to query objects from the database.

Argument.name: object
Argument.Description: The ResourceObject built by the connector.

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: Application that references the connector

Argument.name: connector
Argument.Description: The connector object

Argument.name: A ResourceObject
Argument.Description: The updated resource object.

Rule.name: Example Self-certifier Forward to Manager Rule
Rule.Description: This example falls back to the owner's manager as the new owner of the work item. If the owner has no manager, then fall back to the creator of the certification.

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: The log object associated with the SailPointContext.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: item
Argument.Description: The WorkItem needing to be forwarded. The WorkItem properties may not be fully populated yet.

Argument.name: owner
Argument.Description: The Identity that currently owns the work item.

Argument.name: creator
Argument.Description: The name of identity that created the certification belonging to the work item.

Argument.name: certifiers
Argument.Description: A list of certifier names for the certification belonging to the work item.

Argument.name: name
Argument.Description: The name of the certification belonging to the work item. It may be null if certification is not created yet.

Argument.name: type
Argument.Description: The type of the certification belonging to the work item.

Argument.name: newOwner
Argument.Description: An Identity that should own the work item. Alternatively, this can be a String that is the name or id of the Identity.

Rule.name: Example Policy Owner Rule
Rule.Description: This example simply returns spadmin as an owner for all policies.

Argument.name: environment
Argument.Description: The task arguments passed to the aggregation or refresh task.

Argument.name: policy
Argument.Description: The Policy being evaluated.

Argument.name: constraint
Argument.Description: The Constraint associated with the violation. Provided if available, it is possible for this to be null.

Argument.name: identity

Rule.Source:
import sailpoint.object.Identity;
return context.getObjectByName(Identity.class, "spadmin");

Rule.name: BeforeCreate-Powershell
Rule.Description: This example is for IQService Before Script. This is a sample PowerShell before script which modifies value of an attribute and add one new attribute to the request

Argument.name: log
