Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: plan
Argument.Description: The ProvisioningPlan object on its way to the Connector.

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: The application object that references this before/after script.

Rule.name: AfterCreate-Powershell
Rule.Description: This example is for IQService Before Script. This is a sample PowerShell after script which ensures that the request was processed successfully and creates home directory at the path specified in the request

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: The log object associated with the SailPointContext.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: plan
Argument.Description: The ProvisioningPlan object on its way to the Connector.

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: The application object that references this before/after script.

Rule.name: AfterCreate-Batch
Rule.Description: This example is for IQService Before Script. This is a sample batch after script which ensures that the request was processed successfully and creates home directory at the path specified in the request

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: The log object associated with the SailPointContext.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: plan
Argument.Description: The ProvisioningPlan object on its way to the Connector.

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: The application object that references this before/after script.

Rule.Source: IF %Result% == SUCCESS md %SP_Set_TS_TerminalServicesHomeDirectory%

Rule.name: Example Ticket Plan Generation Rule
Rule.Description: Given the current workflow context, using mostly the identityRequest object build a plan that should represent the ticket data that needs to be pushed into the Remedy Ticket.

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: The log object associated with the SailPointContext.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: wfc
Argument.Description: The currently execution workflow context.

Argument.name: project
Argument.Description: The project that we are dealing with in the provisioning workflow. This argument comes from the workflow context and is here for convenience.

Argument.name: identityRequest
Argument.Description:
The object.IdentityRequest object that contains all of the current request information, including approval and provisioning information depending on where in the process we are being launched.
This argument comes from the workflow context and is here for convenience.

Argument.name: appName
Argument.Description: The name of the application that should handle the ticket management.

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: The application object representing the application that should handle the ticket management.

Rule.name: Example Approval Assignment Rule
Rule.Description:
This example rule switches all of the owners to spadmin. The idea of this rule is to allow some customization of the approvals so the ownership can be cacluated for cases where you might use and exteneded attribute or some other means outside our default mechanisms to derive the owner of the Approval.
This rule is typically configured on the Approval Step in the LCM Provisioning Workflow or can be set on any steps based on the "Provisioning Approval" step library.
Since 6.2

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: The log object associated with the SailPointContext.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: approvals
Argument.Description: List of approvals generated by the approvalScheme, maybe null if there were no approvals enabled.

Argument.name: approvalSet
Argument.Description: Representation of the cart, also found on the approvals generated by the default schemes.

Rule.name: Example Account Selector Rule
Rule.Description:
AccountSelector Rules are used by the role modeler to automatically select a target account for provisioning when there are multiple candidates.
The return value is a Link instance that represents the selection. Value can be:
null - indicates that no automatic selection can be made
"prompt" - indicates that a separate account selector should be presented for the role
existing Link - the selected Link from the list of possible target Links
a new Link instance - indicates that a new account must be created with the identity specified by the create policy, or manually; in this case, the Link instance returned will have a null nativeIdentity attribute

Argument.name: source
Argument.Description: The application doing the provisioning.

Argument.name: assignedRole
Argument.Description: The assigned role being expanded.

Argument.name: role
Argument.Description: The IT role being provisioned that had the assignment rule.

Argument.name: identity
Argument.Description: The target identity.

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: The target application object.

Argument.name: links
Argument.Description: A list of the possible target Links.

Argument.name: isSecondary
Argument.Description: True if this is not the first assignment.

Argument.name: project
Argument.Description: The provisioning project.

Argument.name: accountRequest
Argument.Description: The account request.

Argument.name: allowCreate
Argument.Description: True if account creation is allowed.

Argument.name: selection
Argument.Description: A Link instance that represents the selection. Value can be: null - indicates that no automatic selection can be made Link - the selected Link from the list of possible target Links a new Link instance - indicates that a new account must be created with the identity specified by the create policy, or manually; in this case, the Link instance returned will have a null nativeIdentity attribute

Rule.name: Example SAP HR Custom Manager Model Rule
Rule.Description: Rule to aggregate the supervisor of the employee using custom code. Use this if there is a need to customize determining the supervisor of employees in SAP HR i.e. if the out-of-box supported OOSP and SS models are not in use in SAP-HR. The arguments "employeeID" and "position" can be used to determine the supervisor of the employee.

Argument.name: destination
Argument.Description: A connected and ready to use com.sap.conn.jco.JCoDestination object that can be used to invoke SAP BAPIs using JCo Interface. Use it to call SAP BAPIs and Function Modules. This destination object is shared with the connector implementation and the connector controls the destination's lifecycle.

Argument.name: schema
Argument.Description: The schema that represents the object.

Argument.name: connector
Argument.Description: The SAP connector object being used to communicate with SAP HR target system. The SAPHR Connector Class has many public utility methods which help in invoking SAP Function Modules and lookup SAP tables.

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: The SAP application object being used to connect to the SAP system.

Argument.name: state
Argument.Description: A map object that can be used to store data during the iteration process.

Argument.name: employeeID
Argument.Description: Employee ID of the employee.

Argument.name: objectType
Argument.Description: Object type of the schema(i.e. account).

Argument.name: position
Argument.Description: Position ID of the employee.

Argument.name: supervisor
Argument.Description: Supervisor ID of the employee.

Argument.Description: The PeopleSofthrms connector that is being used to communicate with PeopleSofthrms. This class is here for convenience as there are many utility methods that make calling Function Modules and doing table lookup easier.

Argument.name: session
Argument.Description: The session object.

Argument.name: result
Argument.Description: A Provisioning Result object is desirable to return the status.IT can be a new object or part of Provisioning Plan

Rule.name: Example PeopleSoft HRMS BuildMap Rule
Rule.Description:
This is an example of a buildMap rule for PeopleSoft HRMS that uses a SQL Query call to add additional information to the ResourceObjects built by the default PeopleSoft HRMS Connector implementation.
The rule gets a Map named as map from the buildMap that holds all of the attributes on PeopleSoft HRMS aggregation. The object can be modified/added here to help support customizations and transformations that require PeopleSoft HRMS system.

Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database to aid in provisioning.

Argument.name: application

Argument.Description: Schema representing the data being provisioned.

Argument.name: connection

Argument.name: identity

Argument.name: connector

Argument.name: map
Argument.Description: An connector resorce object map.

Rule.name: Promote SiteAdmin as Entitlement Owner
Rule.Description: This rule is used to set the owner or modify the account group before it is persisted to the database.

Argument.name: log

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.Description: Arguments passed to the aggregation task.

Argument.Description: A sailpoint.object.ResourceObject generated from the application and the ProvisioningPlan's ObjectRequest.

Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.Description: The current request information contain header, body ,response object

Rule.name: Example WebServices AfterRule
Rule.Description: This rule is used by the Web Services connector to update parsed resource object and save connectorStatemap values.Create List of Objects which will later converted to Resource object and save connectorStatemap values to the application object permanently. The returned map will hold key 'data' for resource object list and key 'connectorStateMap' for updated attribute that need to be saved in the application.

Argument.name: log

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: The application whose data file is being processed.

Argument.name: requestEndPoint
Argument.Description: The current request information contain header, body ,response object

Argument.name: processedResponseObject
Argument.Description: Response Object processed by the Web services connector

Argument.name: rawResponseObject
Argument.Description: Response Object returned from the end system

Argument.name: restClient
Argument.Description: REST Client Object

Argument.name: Map which contains updated Account/Group List and connectorStateMap
Argument.Description: Update information Map which contains parsed list of accounts/groups and connectorStateMap

Rule.name: Example WSBeforeRl DropboxPaging
Rule.Description: This rule is used by the Web Services connector before performing any operation like testconnection, aggregation etc.

Argument.name: log

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.Description: The current request information contain header, body, context url, method type, response attribute map, successful response code

Argument.name: provisioningPlan
Argument.Description: provisioningPlan can be used to update the payload of the http request. Provisioning Plan has an account request which defines the operation to be performed on the account. An account request can contain multiple attributes requests and each attribute request represents an operation on a single account attribute.

Argument.name: Endpoint or Updated Map which contains requestEndPoint and connectorStateMap
Argument.Description: Updated Endpoint or information Map which contains endpoint and connectorStateMap

Rule.name: Example Web Services BeforeRule
Rule.Description: This rule is used by the Web Services connector before performing any operation like testconnection, aggregation etc. Here user can update the existing Endpoint object using a key 'updatedEndPoint' and along with it save required attribute in the application using key 'connectorStateMap'. If user just want to return Endpoint (requestEndPoint) no need to create map and update the key values. But if a user wants to return both Endpoint (requestEndPoint) and connectorStateMap who's values will be eventually saved in the application. Create a Map and add both using respective key's.

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: The log object associated with the SailPointContext.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: The application whose data file is being processed.

Argument.name: requestEndPoint
Argument.Description: The current request information contain header, body, context url, method type, response attribute map, successful response code

Argument.name: oldResponseMap
Argument.Description: earlier response object

Argument.name: restClient
Argument.Description: REST Client Object

Argument.name: provisioningPlan
Argument.Description: provisioningPlan can be used to update the payload of the http request. Provisioning Plan has an account request which defines the operation to be performed on the account. An account request can contain multiple attributes requests and each attribute request represents an operation on a single account attribute.

Argument.name: Endpoint or Updated Map which contains requestEndPoint and connectorStateMap
Argument.Description: Updated Endpoint or information Map which contains endpoint and connectorStateMap

Rule.name: WebService SearchAfter Paging example BeforeOperationRule
Rule.Description: The Web Services connector will call this rule before performing ANY defined operation. This rule can be used to add/update values in the endpoint object before performing the operation and/or add persistent values to the application's data. (See information about what to return for more information)

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: The log object associated with the SailPointContext.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: The application associated with the operation being processed.

Argument.name: requestEndPoint
Argument.Description: The current request information containing the header, body, context url, method type, response attribute map, and response code. This object can be modified directly and returned by the rule to update the endpoint information that is used by the current operation.

Argument.name: oldResponseMap
Argument.Description: Earlier response object

Argument.name: restClient
Argument.Description: REST Client Object

Argument.name: provisioningPlan
Argument.Description: A ProvisioningPlan object containing the payload of the http request. A provisioning plan has an account request which defines the operation to be performed on the account. An account request can contain multiple attribute requests and each attribute request represents an operation on a single account attribute.

Argument.name: partition
Argument.Description: If applicable, a Partition object with the current aggregation's partitioning information.

Argument.name: Map
Argument.Description: An updated or unmodified 'requestEndPoint' object. If application object modifications are desired, create a map containing keys 'updatedEndPoint' and 'connectorStateMap' and use it as the return value; Within the new map, the 'updatedEndPoint' can be set to an updated or unmodified 'requestEndPoint' object. The 'connectorStateMap' will be saved as persistent values in the application definition.

Rule.name: WebService SearchAfter Paging example AfterOperationRule
Rule.Description: The Web Services connector will call this rule after performing ANY defined operation. This rule can be used to update the parsed resource object and/or add persistent values to the application's data. (See information about what to return for more information)

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: The log object associated with the SailPointContext.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: The application whose data file is being processed.

Argument.name: requestEndPoint
Argument.Description: The current request information contain header, body ,response object

Argument.name: processedResponseObject
Argument.Description: Response Object processed by the Web services connector

Argument.name: rawResponseObject
Argument.Description: Response Object returned from the end system

Argument.name: restClient
Argument.Description: REST Client Object

Argument.name: Map
Argument.Description: A newly created map containing a 'data' key and optionally, a 'connectorStateMap' key. The 'data' value should be set to a map containing a parsed list of accounts/groups. The 'connectorStateMap' value can be set to a map containing values that will be persisted to the application definition.

Rule.name: Example Attachment Config Rule
Rule.Description: An attachment config rule that uses various input parameters and returns a list of AccessRequestConfigDTO objects

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database.

Argument.name: requester
Argument.Description: The identity making the request.

Argument.name: requestee
Argument.Description: The identity the item is being requested for.

Argument.name: requestedItem
Argument.Description: The requestedItem can be the requested role or entitlement.

Argument.name: action
Argument.Description: 'add' for add requests and 'remove' for 'remove' requests

Argument.name: configList
Argument.Description: Return the list of AccessRequestConfigDTOs to be used for the request.

Rule.name: Example Attachment Config Rule for Roles
Rule.Description: An attachment config rule that uses various inputs and returns a list of AccessRequestConfigDTO objects.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database.

Argument.name: requester
Argument.Description: The identity making the request.

Argument.name: requestee
Argument.Description: The identity the item is being requested for.

Argument.name: requestedItem
Argument.Description: The requestedItem can be the requested role (Bundle) or entitlement (ManagedAttribute).

Argument.name: action
Argument.Description: 'add' for add requests and 'remove' for 'remove' requests

Rule.name: Set Date SAP GRC Role Assignment
Rule.Description: A rule used by a Workflow to change the provisioing project to set startDate and endDate arguments for role.

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: The log object associated with the SailPointContext.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: wfcontext
Argument.Description: The current WorkflowContext.

Argument.name: handler
Argument.Description: The workflow handler associated with the current WorkflowContext.

Argument.name: workflow
Argument.Description: The current Workflow definition.

Argument.name: step
Argument.Description: The current Step.

Argument.name: approval
Argument.Description: The current Approval.

Argument.name: item
Argument.Description: The WorkItem being processed.

Argument.name: Object
Argument.Description: The result of the workflow rule; dependent on the rule itself.

Rule.name: Roles that can be manually removed
Rule.Description: This rule returns a Filter that restricts roles of type where noManualAssignment flag is set to false.

Argument.name: log
Argument.Description: The log object associated with the SailPointContext.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to query the database if necessary.

Argument.name: requestor
Argument.Description: Identity that is making the Life Cycle Manager request.

Argument.name: requestee
Argument.Description: Identity on whose behalf the Life Cycle Manager request is being made. In the case of bulk requests, this argument will be set to null when determining the roles that are visible to the requestor. It will be provided once a selection has been made in order to determine whether or not the given requestee should have access to the selected role.

Argument.name: filter
Argument.Description: A Filter object that will be used to search for accessible Roles.

Rule.name: ExampleAzureActiveDirectoryUsingTenantIdSAML
Native Identity Change Widget Propagation Rule
Rule.Description: An example rule that propagates a list of Native Identity Change Events to all the Widget instances.

Argument.name: events
Argument.Description: A list of Native Identity Change Events.

Argument.name: propagator
Argument.Description: The instance of NativeIdentityChangeGenericPropagator which invoked this rule. It contains some utility methods for tracking ids of objects processed: hasObjectProcessed() markObjectProcessed() Custom propagator rules must use these tracking methods as demonstrated in the example.

Argument.name: taskResult
Argument.Description: The task result.

Argument.name: result
Argument.Description: A boolean result returned by the rule. True indicates that the list of events has been processed successfully.

Rule.name: Example SAP Build map for SailPoint Add On
Rule.Description: This is an example rule which uses SaiLpoint Add On instead of RFC_READ_TABLE. This rule fetches the columns BNAME, LTIME, PWDSTATE and TRDAT for accounts fulfilling the filter criteria passed in where clause. To use SailPoint FM, the fields and tables that are queried will need to be added in /SAILPOIN/CONF. For more information, refer to connector document.

Argument.name: destination
Argument.Description: A connected/ready to use com.sap.conn.jco.JCoDestination object that can be used to call bapi, function modules and call to SAP tables. This is the main object used in making BAPI calls using the JCo interface. This destination object is shared with the connector implementation and the connector controls the destination's lifecycle.

Argument.name: schema
Argument.Description: The schema that represents the object we are building.

Argument.name: connector
Argument.Description: The SAP connector that is being used to communicate with SAP. This class is here for convenience as there are many utility methods that make calling Function Modules and doing table lookup easier

Argument.name: application
Argument.Description: The SAP application object being used to connect to the SAP system.

Argument.name: state
Argument.Description: A map object that can be used to store data during the iteration process.

Argument.name: object
Argument.Description: A map object that holds the attributes that have been built up by the default connector implementation. Any attributes built by this rule should be added to this object. This object is handed by reference, so it can be used directly.

Rule.name: AW - WorkItem Escalation Rule
Rule.Description: escalate any workitems default approver workgroup.

Argument.name: context
Argument.Description: A sailpoint.api.SailPointContext object that can be used to access the database.

Argument.name: item
Argument.Description: The sailpoint.object.Notifiable to escalate.

Argument.name: newOwner
Argument.Description: The name of the Identity object that should be the new owner of the notifiable.

Rule.name: Mover - PAM role Targeted Certification Rule
Rule.Description: This rule generate report Mover - PAM roles Targeted Certication Objects.

Rule.name: AW - Get Classifications
Rule.Description: This rule generate report Mover - PAM roles Targeted Certication Objects.
